record.field: GlitchTip is an open-source, Sentry-compatible error tracking and application monitoring platform for capturing exceptions, releases and alerts.

GlitchTip is an open-source, Sentry-compatible error tracking and application monitoring platform for capturing exceptions, releases and alerts.

GlitchTip is a lightweight, self-hostable alternative to Sentry that speaks the same wire protocol, so any existing Sentry SDK can point at it with just a new DSN. It ingests exceptions and events from your applications, groups them into actionable issues with full stack traces and breadcrumbs, tracks releases and uptime, and notifies your team when something breaks. Built on Django with PostgreSQL and a Celery worker, it targets development teams that want to own their crash-reporting data on their own infrastructure instead of sending stack traces to an external SaaS.

Key features
  • Drop-in compatibility with official Sentry SDKs across every major language and framework
  • Error and exception tracking with stack traces, breadcrumbs and automatic issue grouping
  • Release tracking to spot regressions and tie errors back to a deploy
  • Uptime monitoring and scheduled checks with downtime alerts
  • Alerting by email and outbound webhooks (Slack, Teams, custom endpoints)
  • Multi-organization, multi-team and multi-project isolation with per-project DSNs and configurable event retention
Best for

Development teams that want self-hosted, data-sovereign crash reporting and application error monitoring without a SaaS subscription. Always serve it over HTTPS, keep event retention tuned, and guard your per-project DSNs, since incoming events and stack traces can carry sensitive application data. Configure EMAIL_URL with a real SMTP server to enable alert notifications.

services: postgres: image: postgres:17-alpine restart: unless-stopped environment: POSTGRES_DB: {{ db_name }} POSTGRES_USER: {{ db_user }} POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ db_pass }} healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -q -d $${POSTGRES_DB} -U $${POSTGRES_USER} start_period: 20s interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 volumes: - ./database:/var/lib/postgresql/data expose: - 5432 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' networks: - internal redis: image: valkey/valkey:8-alpine restart: unless-stopped command: - valkey-server - --save - '' - --appendonly - 'no' healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - valkey-cli ping | grep -q PONG start_period: 10s interval: 10s timeout: 3s retries: 5 expose: - 6379 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_redis' networks: - internal web: image: glitchtip/glitchtip:latest restart: unless-stopped command: - ./bin/run-migrate-and-runserver.sh environment: DATABASE_URL: 'postgres://{{ db_user }}:{{ db_pass }}@postgres:5432/{{ db_name }}' SECRET_KEY: '{{ secret_key }}' REDIS_URL: 'redis://redis:6379/0' PORT: '8080' GLITCHTIP_DOMAIN: 'https://{{ instance.full_domain }}' DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL: '{{ default_from_email }}' EMAIL_URL: '{{ email_url }}' GLITCHTIP_MAX_EVENT_LIFE_DAYS: '{{ event_retention_days }}' depends_on: postgres: condition: service_healthy redis: condition: service_healthy volumes: - ./uploads:/code/uploads expose: - 8080 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network worker: image: glitchtip/glitchtip:latest restart: unless-stopped command: - ./bin/run-celery-with-beat.sh environment: DATABASE_URL: 'postgres://{{ db_user }}:{{ db_pass }}@postgres:5432/{{ db_name }}' SECRET_KEY: '{{ secret_key }}' REDIS_URL: 'redis://redis:6379/0' GLITCHTIP_DOMAIN: 'https://{{ instance.full_domain }}' DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL: '{{ default_from_email }}' EMAIL_URL: '{{ email_url }}' GLITCHTIP_MAX_EVENT_LIFE_DAYS: '{{ event_retention_days }}' CELERY_WORKER_AUTOSCALE: '1,3' CELERY_WORKER_MAX_TASKS_PER_CHILD: '10000' depends_on: postgres: condition: service_healthy redis: condition: service_healthy volumes: - ./uploads:/code/uploads container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_worker' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump lógico consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR. # Con POSTGRES_USER/DB vacíos caemos a 'glitchtip' (default del template), evitando # que pg_dump intente el rol "root" del SO y falle con «role "root" does not exist». docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "${POSTGRES_USER:-glitchtip}" -d "${POSTGRES_DB:-${POSTGRES_USER:-glitchtip}}" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura (mismo default a 'glitchtip') docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "${POSTGRES_USER:-glitchtip}"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "${POSTGRES_USER:-glitchtip}" -d "${POSTGRES_DB:-${POSTGRES_USER:-glitchtip}}" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \${POSTGRES_USER:-glitchtip} -d \${POSTGRES_DB:-glitchtip} -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
PostgreSQL database that stores GlitchTip data.
Django SECRET_KEY used for cryptographic signing.
From address used for alert and invitation emails.
Outbound email backend URL. Defaults to console output; set an SMTP URL like smtp://user:pass@smtp.example.com:587 to send real alerts.
Number of days GlitchTip keeps ingested events before pruning them.
